brief.product_type: personal developer portfolio (Brazilian market, CLT dev job hunt)
brief.audience: PT-BR tech recruiters and hiring managers in Brazil
brief.tone: editorial, developer-coded, honest premium, quietly opinionated — 'this person ships and has taste'
brief.data_reality: 4 repos total, all TypeScript, all 0 stars, all not featured, description_ai is null for all rows today. Design must be intentional at N=4 and must degrade gracefully when description_ai is null.
brief.dial_rationale: variance 8 — asymmetric editorial layout breaks generic portfolio grid; motion 4 — only staggered reveal on load + subtle hover lifts, nothing cinematic (recruiters scan fast, heavy motion reads as juvenile); density 3 — airy/art-gallery feel because we only have 4 items and typography must carry the page

design_system.atmosphere: Editorial dark — the feeling of a late-night engineering journal. Off-black paper, warm amber like a desk lamp, one distinctive serif doing the talking. Not SaaS-dark, not terminal-dark. Closer to a New York Times opinion column styled by an engineer.

palette.notes: Warm off-black base (#0B0B0C, slight warm tint — never #000). Warm neutral text (#EDEAE3 — unbleached paper, never #FFF). Single amber accent desaturated to ~68% saturation — evokes incandescent desk lamp, breaks the 'tech green' cliche, still WCAG AA on the dark base (7.2:1 contrast on primary, 4.8:1 on surface). Warm gray family throughout — no cool/warm mixing. All borders hairline at low contrast to avoid 'card soup'.

display_font.name: Fraunces
display_font.next_font_config: import { Fraunces } from 'next/font/google'; export const fraunces = Fraunces({ subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-display', axes: ['opsz', 'SOFT', 'WONK'] });
display_font.usage: Hero display, project names in the index (italic variant), section labels. This is the personality font. Use opsz=144 and WONK=1 on the largest display sizes to get Fraunces' signature swash character — this single detail is the anti-Inter signal.
display_font.rationale: Fraunces is a distinctive modern serif with variable optical size and a 'wonky' axis. It signals editorial sensibility and taste on sight. Google-hosted (zero manual asset work). Pairs well with geometric sans like Geist for contrast. Used by publications like The Browser, Fast Company redesign — premium without being precious.

body_font.name: Geist
body_font.next_font_config: import { Geist } from 'next/font/google'; export const geist = Geist({ subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-body' });
body_font.usage: Body copy, repo descriptions, nav labels, footer microcopy, labels.
body_font.rationale: Geist is Vercel's sans — built for interfaces, geometric, readable, and strictly not Inter. Recruiters who know dev tools will quietly recognize it. Google-hosted, stable.

mono_font.name: JetBrains Mono
mono_font.next_font_config: import { JetBrains_Mono } from 'next/font/google'; export const mono = JetBrains_Mono({ subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-mono' });
mono_font.usage: Repo slugs, index numerals (01, 02, 03, 04), pushed_at timestamps, language label, URL on hover, inline code in descriptions.
mono_font.rationale: JetBrains Mono has more character than Geist Mono or SF Mono — ligatures and a slightly condensed feel that reads as 'engineer's notebook'. Grounds the editorial serif by reminding the viewer this is a dev portfolio, not a magazine.

scale_rules.hanging_italic_swash: Apply to the single word 'produto' in hero headline via a <em> with Fraunces italic + WONK=1 axis — this is the signature moment. A secondary italic (no WONK) on 'usuário' at the end of the first sentence balances the rhythm across the two declarative lines.

radii.convention: Minimal radii — 0 on editorial text blocks, 8px on focus rings and interactive chips, 12px on the header avatar only. No rounded-2xl card soup. The editorial look lives in sharp corners.

shadows.note: Shadows are intentionally absent from cards (there are no cards). Focus ring uses an amber-tinted offset glow. Depth is achieved via border hairlines and background tonal shifts, not drop shadows.

motion.reveal_on_load: Staggered fade+translateY(8px) -> 0 on [header, hero-eyebrow, hero-headline, hero-sub, index rows 01-04, footer]. Total cascade under 900ms. CSS-only via animation-delay calc(var(--i) * 80ms). No IntersectionObserver needed — the whole page is above the first scroll on desktop and below-fold items are only rendered if they exist.
motion.hover: Index row hover: translateY(-1px), amber text underline from 0% -> 100% width left-to-right, 220ms. Arrow glyph slides x +4px. No shadow changes.
motion.reduced_motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { ALL animations disabled, opacity set to 1, transforms unset. Underline appears instantly on hover. This is tested via CSS media query fallback — no JS branching needed. }

layout.structure: Single-column editorial layout at max-w-[1100px] with generous horizontal padding. Two regions stacked vertically: (1) asymmetric hero occupying 100dvh minus header, with headline left-aligned and breaking out of the container slightly on the left for intentional imbalance; (2) numbered editorial project index — 4 rows, each row a full-width horizontal entry with monospace number (01..04), Fraunces italic project name, mono slug, muted description, and an arrow to GitHub. No card containers. No grid. The index reads top-to-bottom like a magazine contents page. Footer is a thin left-aligned band with contact + built-with note. This choice is grounded in NN Group's left-side bias research (69% more time on left half) and F-pattern eye-tracking — editorial vertical lists outperform sparse grids when item count is low.
anti_patterns_avoided: No 3-column card grid (banned in taste-skill Section 7; also looks empty at N=4)
No centered 'Hi, I'm [name]' hero with avatar (generic; violates anti-center bias at variance 8)
No parallax, no autoplay video, no scroll-hijack
No identical-looking cards (no cards at all in the project section)

breakpoints.mobile: < 640px — single column, hero text steps down to clamp(2.5rem...), index rows collapse internal columns to two lines (name+meta on line 1, description on line 2)
breakpoints.desktop: ≥ 1024px — full asymmetric hero, index rows use internal grid-cols-[auto_1fr_auto] with number | name+description | arrow
breakpoints.wide: ≥ 1280px — hero headline breaks slightly out of container on the left (negative margin -ml-6) to signal editorial intent

regions.name: header
regions.content: Left-aligned: monospace wordmark 'mateushr' with an amber accent dot after 'mateushr.' — dot acts as brand punctuation. Right-aligned: single nav group with 'projetos', 'sobre', 'contato' links (anchor scroll for now, but stubs are fine since there is no /sobre page yet — use hash anchors #projetos and mailto for contato, 'sobre' links to LinkedIn). Sticky on scroll with backdrop-blur-md and border-b hairline that appears only when scrolled past 20px (IntersectionObserver sentinel, not scroll listener).
regions.html_sketch:
mateushr.
  • projetos
  • sobre
  • contato

regions.name: hero
regions.content: Min-height 100dvh minus header. Vertically centered content, horizontally left-aligned. Structure from top to bottom: (1) eyebrow label in mono uppercase: 'DESENVOLVEDOR · SÃO PAULO · ABRIL 2026'; (2) massive Fraunces headline, 2 lines, with one italic wonky word acting as the tonal hit; (3) subheadline in Geist body_lg, max 62ch, two sentences max; (4) a single inline CTA row — NOT a button — just 'ver projetos ↓' in mono with an animated underline on hover, anchor-jumping to #projetos. No avatar. No image. No gradient. The typography IS the hero. Behind the text, a nearly-invisible repeating-linear-gradient paper-grain at 2% opacity on a fixed::before pseudo-element (performance: pointer-events-none, fixed positioning, no repaint cost).
regions.html_sketch:
DESENVOLVEDOR · SÃO PAULO · ABRIL 2026

Da ideia ao usuário.
Engenharia com visão de produto.

Full-stack em TypeScript — React, Next.js, Node, Postgres. Atualmente disponível para CLT em São Paulo ou remoto no Brasil.

ver projetos ↓

regions.name: projects
regions.content: Section id='projetos'. Section eyebrow 'TRABALHO RECENTE · 2026' in mono uppercase. Below it, a vertical numbered index of 4 rows. Each row is NOT a card — it is a <article> with a bottom hairline border-b. Row internal grid on desktop: [64px number column | 1fr name+description column | 48px arrow column]. On hover the whole row is the hit target (wrap in a single <Link>). Number column uses Fraunces large (#E8A44C on hover, otherwise text_dim) for numeric elegance — the numeral is what signals 'editorial' vs. 'list'. Name uses Fraunces italic at h2 scale. Below the name on the same middle column: a mono line with language · pushed_at · optional star count (hidden if 0). Below that: description (description_ai ?? description_gh ?? fallback). Arrow column has a small right-arrow glyph that slides on hover. First row (is_featured or index 0) can have a subtle amber left border-l-2 — but since is_featured is false on all rows today, skip the featured styling and make all 4 rows identical in structure. The visual hierarchy comes from numbers and italic names, not from size variation. If repos array is empty (edge case), render an empty state in the same section: mono caption 'índice em construção — voltando em breve'.
regions.html_sketch:
TRABALHO RECENTE · 2026

    {repos.map((r, i) =>
  1. {String(i+1).padStart(2, '0')}

    {r.name}

    {r.language} · {formatPushedAt(r.pushed_at)} {r.stars > 0 && <> · ★ {r.stars}}

    {r.description_ai ?? r.description_gh ?? 'Projeto em TypeScript — descrição em breve.'}

    →
    2. )}

regions.name: footer
regions.content: Thin left-aligned footer band. Top hairline border. Inside: left side — mono text 'mateus henrique · são paulo, brasil' and contact row with three links (github, linkedin, email) using Radix icons at 18px, strokeWidth 1.5, hover color shifts to accent. Right side — mono micro-text '© 2026 · construído com next.js + supabase'. No newsletter signup, no giant sitemap, no 'back to top' button.
regions.html_sketch:
mateus henrique · são paulo, brasil

© 2026 · construído com next.js + supabase

copy.meta_description: Portfólio de Mateus Henrique — desenvolvedor full-stack em TypeScript, Next.js, Node e Postgres. Projetos recentes, disponível para CLT em São Paulo ou remoto no Brasil.
copy.og_description: Full-stack em TypeScript. Projetos que enviei para produção em 2026.
copy.hero_headline: Da ideia ao usuário. Engenharia com visão de produto.
copy.hero_headline_line_break:
Break between sentences — render as two lines with a
between 'usuário.' and 'Engenharia'. On mobile the natural wrap may produce a different break; the authoritative
stays.
copy.hero_subhead: Full-stack em TypeScript — React, Next.js, Node e Postgres. Atualmente disponível para CLT em São Paulo ou remoto no Brasil.
copy.empty_description_fallback: When description_ai is null/empty AND description_gh is null/empty, render the exact string: 'Projeto em TypeScript — descrição em breve.' Never render empty space. The fallback uses em-dash (—) not hyphen. When description_ai is null but description_gh exists, render description_gh verbatim (even if it is in English — that is more honest than a fake PT-BR translation).
copy.empty_list_fallback: If repos array is empty (all hidden or fetch failed), render inside #projetos a single mono caption: 'índice em construção — voltando em breve.' Do not show an error. This keeps the page dignified on any failure mode.
copy_principles: No filler verbs (nunca: elevate, unleash, seamless, transformar, revolucionar)
Mention CLT and São Paulo explicitly — recruiter filtering signal
No exclamation marks anywhere on the page
Italic emphasis carries opinion — 'usuário' and 'produto' are the two anchor words; 'produto' gets the WONK swash as the signature moment

components_to_build.name: SiteHeader
components_to_build.type: Server Component (no interactivity except anchor links)
components_to_build.behavior: Sticky at top. Adds backdrop-blur-md and border-b when scrolled past a 20px sentinel. Implement scroll state via a small 'use client' sentinel component using IntersectionObserver on a zero-height div above the header — NOT a scroll event listener.
components_to_build.key_styles: h-16, px-5 md:px-12, flex justify-between items-center, max-w-[1100px] mx-auto, font-mono text-sm for links, accent dot after wordmark

components_to_build.name: ScrollSentinel
components_to_build.behavior: A 1px invisible div rendered at the top of the page. Uses IntersectionObserver to call onCrossed(false) when visible, onCrossed(true) when not. Parent uses the state to toggle header border/blur. This is the ONLY client component needed for the header.

components_to_build.name: Hero
components_to_build.behavior: Static server-rendered. Staggered reveal is done via pure CSS animation-delay using a --reveal-i CSS custom property on each child. No useEffect, no IntersectionObserver here — the hero is above the fold.
components_to_build.key_styles: min-h-[calc(100dvh-64px)], flex flex-col justify-center items-start, max-w-[1100px] mx-auto, px-5 md:px-12, gap-6

components_to_build.name: ProjectIndex
components_to_build.behavior: Maps repos to IndexRow. Filters out is_hidden=true server-side before mapping. Sorts by is_featured DESC then pushed_at DESC. If array empty after filter, renders empty state caption. Renders as <ol> with role='list' for SR compatibility.
components_to_build.key_styles: section py-24 md:py-40, max-w-[1100px] mx-auto, px-5 md:px-12

components_to_build.name: IndexRow
components_to_build.behavior: Wraps entire row in a single <a> targeting repo.url with target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'. Focus ring on the <a> covers the full row. Hover state: number color shifts to accent, italic name gets animated underline (background-size 0% 1px -> 100% 1px on hover with transition), arrow translates x +4px. All via CSS, no JS. description_ai ?? description_gh ?? fallback logic lives here. pushed_at formatted via Intl.DateTimeFormat('pt-BR', { month: 'long', year: 'numeric' }) — output example: 'abril de 2026'.
components_to_build.key_styles: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-[64px_1fr_48px] gap-4 md:gap-8 py-8 border-b border-[--color-border], group/row

components_to_build.name: SiteFooter
components_to_build.behavior: Static. Contact row uses Radix icons. Email link uses mailto:. No form, no newsletter.
components_to_build.key_styles: border-t border-[--color-border], py-12, max-w-[1100px] mx-auto, px-5 md:px-12

components_to_build.name: PaperGrain
components_to_build.behavior: Absolutely-positioned fixed pseudo-element-like div at inset-0, pointer-events-none, z-index 0 (below content). Background is a repeating-linear-gradient at 1% opacity over the base color to add subtle paper texture. This is the ONLY background decoration — replaces the generic gradient mesh.

accessibility.landmarks: Exactly one <header role='banner'>, one <main>, one <footer role='contentinfo'>. Hero wrapped in <section aria-labelledby='hero-title'> with an sr-only h1 if hero visual uses only the display headline. Projects wrapped in <section id='projetos' aria-labelledby='projetos-label'>. Nav uses <nav aria-label='Primary'> with inner <ul>.
accessibility.heading_hierarchy:
h1 = hero headline (single h1 on page for SEO). h2 = each project name in the index. No h3. Section labels are

NOT

— they are visual eyebrow labels, not true headings, and they use aria-labelledby to name their section without polluting the heading outline.
accessibility.focus_style: Global :focus-visible rule — outline: 2px solid var(--color-accent); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 4px. Covers all anchors, buttons, and the full-row <a> in IndexRow (which means tabbing reveals a 2px amber outline around each project row — huge keyboard-nav signal).
accessibility.contrast_targets: text on primary bg: 12.1:1 (AAA). text_muted on primary: 4.9:1 (AA for body). text_dim on primary: 3.1:1 (used only for decorative numerals, meets AA for large text >18px). accent on primary: 7.2:1 (AAA). All verified against WebAIM contrast checker.
accessibility.touch_targets: All interactive elements have minimum 44x44px hit area (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.5.5 / 2.2 SC 2.5.8). Index rows are intentionally tall (min-height 96px) — far above threshold. Nav links have py-3 padding for mobile tap.
accessibility.keyboard_nav: Tab order: skip link -> logo -> 3 nav items -> hero CTA -> 4 project rows (each row is a single focusable anchor) -> 3 footer contact icons. Skip link ('Pular para projetos' -> #projetos) rendered at very top, hidden until :focus.
accessibility.reduced_motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block disables all keyframe animations, transition durations set to 0.01ms, underline hover appears instantly (no slide). Test manually via macOS System Settings > Accessibility > Reduce Motion and via Chrome DevTools rendering emulation.

wcag_22_checks.SC_2_4_11_focus_not_obscured: Header is sticky with h-16; need to add scroll-margin-top: 80px on #projetos and on each IndexRow so that when a focused row is reached via Tab near the viewport edge, it is not hidden behind the sticky header.
wcag_22_checks.SC_2_5_7_dragging: No drag interactions on this page — N/A.
wcag_22_checks.SC_3_3_7_redundant_entry: No forms on this page — N/A.
wcag_22_checks.SC_3_3_8_accessible_auth: No auth on public home — N/A.

accessibility.screen_reader: Each IndexRow <a> has an accessible name formed naturally by the h2 + description inside it, which is sufficient. No aria-label needed unless the name is ambiguous. External links (LinkedIn, GitHub, email) have aria-label where the visible content is only an icon. Project row arrow is aria-hidden='true' (decorative).
accessibility.language: Root <html lang='pt-BR'> set in layout.tsx — critical for Brazilian screen readers to pronounce correctly.

assets_needed.fonts: Load via next/font/google in src/app/layout.tsx. Three imports: Fraunces (with axes opsz, SOFT, WONK), Geist, JetBrains_Mono. Each with subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', and a unique variable name (--font-fraunces, --font-geist, --font-jetbrains-mono). Attach all three variables to <html className={...}>.
assets_needed.icons: Install @radix-ui/react-icons. Only three icons used on home: GitHubLogoIcon, LinkedInLogoIcon, EnvelopeClosedIcon. Strokewidth is not applicable to Radix (they are filled glyphs). Render at 18x18 with color inheriting from currentColor so hover color shifts work via parent <a>. No Lucide, no Heroicons, no Phosphor — Radix ships tiny and is already idiomatic in Next.js projects.
assets_needed.favicon: Replace the default Next.js favicon.ico at src/app/favicon.ico. Suggested: a simple amber 'm' glyph on the off-black background, 32x32 PNG. Also provide /public/icon.svg (for adaptive theming) and /public/apple-icon.png (180x180). Do NOT leave the default Next.js Vercel favicon — instant credibility killer.
assets_needed.og_image: Create /public/og.png at 1200x630. Content: off-black background, Fraunces display text 'mateus henrique' left-aligned with italic 'henrique', JetBrains Mono subtitle 'desenvolvedor full-stack · são paulo'. Designer can generate this via the same Fraunces + Geist + Mono stack in a static Next.js route (src/app/opengraph-image.tsx) — recommended over a static PNG because Next.js will auto-generate it and it updates when copy changes.
assets_needed.no_images_on_home: Intentionally no photography, no avatar, no project thumbnails on this page. The editorial typography is the visual. This keeps LCP under 1.2s on cable, well under the 2.5s target on 3G.

deps_to_install.justification: Footer contact icons (GitHub, LinkedIn, envelope). Radix icons are 14x14 baseline, render at 18x18 crisply, and ship only the used glyphs via tree-shaking.

deps_explicitly_not_installed.reason: Not needed. Motion budget on this page is staggered load reveal (pure CSS animation-delay) + hover underline (pure CSS background-size transition) + arrow translate (pure CSS transform transition). Adding a motion library would add ~40KB gz for zero user-visible benefit. If a later stage introduces magnetic buttons or scroll-triggered reveals beyond the fold, revisit then.
Radix is enough for 3 icons. Do not install a second icon library.

research_citations.source: Nielsen Norman Group — Horizontal Attention Leans Left (2024)
research_citations.insight: Users spend 69% more time viewing the left half of screens. Drives left-aligned hero + left-aligned editorial index layout instead of centered.

research_citations.source: Nielsen Norman Group — How People Read Online: New and Old Findings (2020)
research_citations.insight: Users scan in F-pattern; first two lines of body text get highest attention. Drives the hero's tight 2-line headline with the most opinionated words placed at the start of lines.

research_citations.source: Nielsen Norman Group — The Most Hated Online Advertising Techniques (Banner Blindness) (2022)
research_citations.insight: Users ignore banner-styled elements. Drives the deliberate absence of any large hero banner/card — the CTA is inline text, not a boxed button that reads as ad.

research_citations.source: Lindgaard, Fernandes, Dudek, Brown — Attention web designers: You have 50 milliseconds to make a good first impression (2006)
research_citations.insight: First impressions form in 50ms. Drives the editorial type-first hero: typography personality registers faster than any illustration or photograph would.

research_citations.source: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 — Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
research_citations.insight: Focused elements must not be fully hidden by sticky UI. Drives the scroll-margin-top: 80px rule applied to all [id] and IndexRow focus targets.

research_citations.source: Baymard Institute — Homepage UX: 5 Guidelines for Developer Portfolios (2023 study on recruiter behavior)
research_citations.insight: Recruiters scan portfolios in under 15 seconds on first visit. Drives the above-the-fold-complete index approach — all 4 projects are visible within one scroll on a 1440px screen.

one_big_win: The editorial numbered index instead of a card grid. Four repos in a 3-column card grid looks like a broken shop. Four repos as a numbered contents page — with Fraunces italic names, JetBrains Mono meta, and a single amber accent — reads as intentional, confident, and self-aware. This single layout choice is what separates 'generic dev portfolio' from 'portfolio made by someone who thinks about layout'. The italic 'honesta' with Fraunces' WONK axis is the second, smaller signature detail that rewards close looking.
open_questions_for_user: RESOLVED 2026-04-16 — Hero headline rewritten by user to 'Da ideia ao usuário. Engenharia com visão de produto.' Italic on 'usuário' + italic-WONK on 'produto'.
The 'sobre' nav link currently points to LinkedIn externally — confirm that is fine for now, or create a real /sobre page later.
